post.description: Adds an email to your email rejection allowlist. If the address is currently on your denylist, that denylist entry will be removed automatically.
Retrieves your email rejection allowlist. You can provide an email address or search prefix to limit the results. Returns up to 1000 results.
Removes an email address from the allowlist.
Returns information about an export job including status and download URL when complete.
Returns a list of all export jobs for the account.
Begins an export of your rejection blacklist. The blacklist will be exported to a zip archive containing a single file named rejects.csv.
Begins an export of your rejection allowlist. The allowlist will be exported to a zip archive containing a single file named allowlist.csv.
Begins an export of your rejection allowlist. The allowlist will be exported to a zip archive containing a single file named allowlist.csv.
Begins an export of your activity history. The activity will be exported to a zip archive containing a single file named activity.csv with message details and custom metadata fields.
Lists your dedicated IPs.
Retrieves information about a single dedicated IP.
Requests an additional dedicated IP for your account. Accounts may have one outstanding request at any time, and provisioning requests are processed within 24 hours.

Begins the warmup process for a dedicated IP. During the warmup process, Mandrill will gradually increase the percentage of your mail that is sent over the warming-up IP, over a period of roughly 30 days. The rest of your mail will be sent over shared IPs or other dedicated IPs in the same pool.
Cancels the warmup process for a dedicated IP.
Moves a dedicated IP to a different pool.
Deletes a dedicated IP. This is permanent and cannot be undone.
Lists your dedicated IP pools.
Describes a single dedicated IP pool.
Creates a pool and returns it. If a pool already exists with this name, no action will be performed.
Deletes a pool. A pool must be empty before you can delete it, and you cannot delete your default pool.
Tests whether a domain name is valid for use as the custom reverse DNS for a dedicated IP.
Configures the custom DNS name for a dedicated IP.
List the domains that have been configured for inbound delivery.
Add an inbound domain to your account.
Check the MX settings for an inbound domain. The domain must have already been added with the add-domain call.
Delete an inbound domain from the account. All mail will stop routing for this domain immediately.
List the mailbox routes defined for an inbound domain.
Add a new mailbox route to an inbound domain.
Update the pattern or webhook of an existing inbound mailbox route. If null is provided for any fields, the values will remain unchanged.
Delete an existing inbound mailbox route.
Take a raw MIME document destined for a domain with inbound domains set up, and send it to the inbound hook exactly as if it had been sent over SMTP.
Returns complete information about a Mailchimp Transactional template including draft and published versions
Returns a list of all Mailchimp Transactional templates for the authenticated user
Renders a Mailchimp Transactional template with optional merge variables

post.summary: Get Mailchimp Transactional template time series
post.description: Returns hourly stats for the last 30 days for a Mailchimp Transactional template. Returns an empty array if the template exists but has never been used to send messages.

post.summary: Send a new transactional message through the Transactional API.
post.description:
Send a new transactional message through Mandrill
Note
If you signed up for a Mandrill account on or after December 1st, 2015, you must add SPF and DKIM records and verify ownership of your sending domains before you can send email through your account. Mandrill will not send any email from unverified domains or domains without valid SPF and DKIM records, including public domains like gmail.com, yahoo.com, and more.

Custom signing domains are only available to Mandrill accounts created before December 1st, 2015. If you signed up for a new Mandrill account on or after December 1st, 2015, the signing_domain parameter will be ignored.

Mail sent from unverified domains or domains without valid SPF and DKIM records will be rejected with the reject_reason, unsigned.

Learn more about SPF and DKIM, and domain verification

post.summary: Send a new transactional message using a template
post.description: Send a new transactional message using a template. If you signed up for a Mandrill account on or after December 1st, 2015, you must add SPF and DKIM records and verify ownership of your sending domains before you can send email through your account. Mandrill will not send any email from unverified domains or domains without valid SPF and DKIM records, including public domains like gmail.com, yahoo.com, and more. Custom signing domains are only available to Mandrill accounts created before December 1st, 2015. If you signed up for a new Mandrill account on or after December 1st, 2015, the signing_domain parameter will be ignored. Mail sent from unverified domains or domains without valid SPF and DKIM records will be rejected with the reject_reason, unsigned.
Send a new transactional message through Mandrill using a Mailchimp Transactional template.

Search recently sent messages and optionally narrow by date range, tags, senders, and API keys. Results can include both email and SMS messages. If no date range is specified, results within the last 7 days are returned.

post.summary: Get time series data for message search
post.description: Search the content of recently sent messages and return the aggregated hourly stats for matching messages.

post.summary: Get information about a single sent message
post.description: Returns detailed information about a single sent message. Can return either email or SMS message information depending on the message type.

post.summary: Get the full content of a sent message
post.description: Returns the full content of a recently sent message. For email messages, includes HTML, text, and attachments. For SMS messages, includes text content and SMS-specific fields.
Parse the full MIME document for an email message, returning the content of the message broken into its constituent pieces.

post.summary: Send a raw MIME message through Mandrill
post.description: Send a raw MIME document for a message, and send it exactly as if it were sent through Mandrill's SMTP servers.
Queries your scheduled emails.
Cancels a scheduled email.
Reschedules a scheduled email.
Send a new SMS message through Mandrill
Get the list of custom metadata fields indexed for the account
Add a new custom metadata field to be indexed for the account
Update an existing custom metadata field
Delete an existing custom metadata field. Deletion is not instantaneous, and /metadata/list will continue to return the field until the asynchronous deletion process is complete.

Adds an email to your email rejection denylist. Addresses that you add manually will never expire and there is no reputation penalty for removing them from your denylist. Attempting to denylist an address that has been whitelisted will have no effect.

post.summary: Add phone number to SMS rejection denylist
post.description: Adds a phone number to your SMS rejection denylist. Phone numbers that you add manually will never expire and there is no reputation penalty for removing them from your denylist.

Retrieves your email rejection denylist. You can provide an email address to limit the results. Returns up to 1000 results. By default, entries that have expired are excluded from the results; set include_expired to true to include them.

Retrieves your SMS rejection denylist. You can provide a phone number to limit the results. Returns up to 1000 results. By default, entries that have expired are excluded from the results; set include_expired to true to include them.
Deletes an email rejection. There is no limit to how many rejections you can remove from your denylist, but keep in mind that each deletion has an affect on your reputation.

post.summary: Delete phone number from SMS rejection denylist
post.description: Deletes an SMS rejection. There is no limit to how many rejections you can remove from your denylist, but keep in mind that each deletion has an affect on your reputation.

post.summary: List senders used by this account
post.description: Return the senders that have tried to use this account.
Returns the sender domains that have been added to this account.
Adds a sender domain to your account. Sender domains are added automatically as you send, but you can add them ahead of time.
Checks the SPF and DKIM settings for a domain. If it isn't already added to your account, it will be added automatically.

post.summary: Verify a sender domain by email
post.description: Sends a verification email in order to verify ownership of a domain.

post.summary: Get detailed information for a sender
post.description: Return more detailed information about a single sender, including aggregates of recent stats.

post.summary: Get hourly stats for a sender
post.description: Return the recent history (hourly stats for the last 30 days) for a sender.
Deletes an unverified sender domain from your account. Verified domains cannot be deleted via API and require login confirmation.
Get the list of subaccounts defined for the account, optionally filtered by a prefix. Returns up to a maximum of 1,000 subaccounts.
Create a new subaccount with the specified configuration. The subaccount can be used to organize and track sending for different customers or purposes.
Retrieve detailed information about a specific subaccount including sending statistics and configuration.
Update the configuration of an existing subaccount including name, notes, and custom quota settings.
Delete an existing subaccount. Any email related to the subaccount will be saved, but stats will be removed and any future sending calls to this subaccount will fail.
Pause a subaccount's sending. Any future emails delivered to this subaccount will be queued for a maximum of 3 days until the subaccount is resumed.
Resume a paused subaccount's sending. The subaccount will return to active status and queued emails will be processed.
Return all of the user-defined tag information
Deletes a tag permanently. Deleting a tag removes the tag from any messages that have been sent, and also deletes the tag's stats. There is no way to undo this operation, so use it carefully.

post.summary: Get detailed information about a tag
post.description: Return more detailed information about a single tag, including aggregates of recent stats

post.summary: Get time series data for a specific tag
post.description: Return the recent history (hourly stats for the last 30 days) for a tag

post.summary: Get time series data for all tags
post.description: Return the recent history (hourly stats for the last 30 days) for all tags
Creates a new email template with the specified content and settings.
Retrieves information for an existing template.
Updates the code for an existing template. If null is provided for any fields, the values will remain unchanged.
Publishes the content for the template. Any new messages sent using this template will start using the content that was previously in draft.
Deletes an existing template.
Returns a list of all templates available to this user.
Returns the recent history (hourly stats for the last 30 days) for a template.
Injects content and merge variables into a template, returning the HTML that results.
Get the list of tracking domains set up for your account
Add a tracking domain to your account
Checks the CNAME settings for a tracking domain. The domain must have been added already with the add-tracking-domain call
Deletes an unverified tracking domain from your account. Valid tracking domains cannot be deleted via API and require login confirmation.

post.summary: Return the information about the API-connected user
post.description: Returns user account information including username, reputation, quota, and historical sending stats

post.summary: Validate an API key and respond to a ping
post.description: Returns 'PONG!'.

post.summary: Validate an API key and respond to a ping (strict JSON parser version)
post.description: Validate an API key and respond to a ping (strict JSON parser version)

post.summary: Return the senders that have tried to use this account
post.description: Returns an array of sender data for each sending address used by the account, both verified and unverified

post.summary: Get the list of all webhooks
post.description: Get the list of all webhooks defined for this account
Create a new webhook to receive event notifications
Get the data about an existing webhook
Update the configuration of an existing webhook
Remove a webhook from the account

MergeVar.title: MergeVar
MergeVar.description: Merge variable for template content injection

name.description: Merge variable name (case-insensitive, may not start with _)

content.description: Content value for the merge variable

Sender.description: Information about a sending address used by the account.

sent.description: Total messages sent by this sender

hard_bounces.description: Total number of hard bounces by this sender

soft_bounces.description: Total number of soft bounces by this sender

rejects.description: Total number of rejected messages by this sender

complaints.description: Total number of spam complaints for this sender

unsubs.description: Total number of unsubscribe requests for this sender

opens.description: Total number of times messages by this sender have been opened

clicks.description: Total number of tracked URL clicks by this sender

unique_clicks.description: Number of unique clicks for emails sent by this sender

unique_opens.description: Number of unique opens for emails sent by this sender

StatsWindow.description: Aggregate sending statistics for a time window

hard_bounces.description: Number of emails that hard bounced

soft_bounces.description: Number of emails that soft bounced

opens.description: Number of times emails were opened

AllowlistsAddRequest.description: Request payload for adding an email to the allowlist

email.description: The email address to remove from the allowlist

deleted.description: Whether the address was deleted successfully

detail.description: A description of why the email was allowlisted

created_at.description: When the email was added to the allowlist

ExportsActivityRequest.description: Request parameters for exporting activity history with optional filters

notify_email.description: Optional email address to notify when the export job has finished

date_from.description: Start date as a UTC string in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

date_to.description: End date as a UTC string in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

tags.description: Array of tag names to narrow the export to; will match messages that contain ANY of the tags

senders.description: Array of sender email addresses to narrow the export to

states.description: Array of message states to narrow the export to; messages with ANY of the states will be included

api_keys.description: Array of API keys to narrow the export to; messages sent with ANY of the keys will be included

items.description: An API key associated with your account

ExportsAllowlistRequest.description: Request payload for exporting the rejection allowlist

notify_email.description: Optional email address to notify when the export job has finished

Export.description: Export job information including status and download URL when complete

id.description: Unique identifier for this export job

created_at.description: Date and time the export job was created in UTC YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

finished_at.description: Date and time the export job finished in UTC YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format, or null if not finished

state.description: Current state of the export job

result_url.description: URL to download the export results when job is complete, or null if not ready

ExportsInfoRequest.description: Request payload for retrieving export job information

id.description: Export job identifier

ExportsListRequest.description: Request payload for listing export jobs

ExportsListResponse.description: List of export jobs for the account

ExportsRejectsRequest.description: Request payload for exporting the rejection blacklist

notify_email.description: Optional email address to notify when the export job has finished

ExportsWhitelistRequest.description: Request payload for exporting the rejection allowlist (legacy endpoint)

notify_email.description: Optional email address to notify when the export job has finished

IP.description: Information about a dedicated IP address including warmup status and custom DNS configuration

ips.description: The dedicated IPs in this pool

email.description: The email address of the recipient

name.description: The optional display name to use for the recipient

type.description: the header type to use for the recipient, defaults to "to" if not provided Possible values: "to", "cc", or "bcc".

headers.description: optional extra headers to add to the message (most headers are allowed)

important.description: whether or not this message is important, and should be delivered ahead of non-important messages

track_opens.description: whether or not to turn on open tracking for the message

track_clicks.description: whether or not to turn on click tracking for the message

auto_text.description: whether or not to automatically generate a text part for messages that are not given text

auto_html.description: whether or not to automatically generate an HTML part for messages that are not given HTML

inline_css.description: whether or not to automatically inline all CSS styles provided in the message HTML - only for HTML documents less than 256KB in size

url_strip_qs.description: whether or not to strip the query string from URLs when aggregating tracked URL data

preserve_recipients.description: whether or not to expose all recipients in to "To" header for each email

view_content_link.description: set to false to remove content logging for sensitive emails

bcc_address.description: an optional address to receive an exact copy of each recipient's email

tracking_domain.description: a custom domain to use for tracking opens and clicks instead of mandrillapp.com

signing_domain.description: a custom domain to use for SPF/DKIM signing instead of mandrill (for "via" or "on behalf of" in email clients)

return_path_domain.description: a custom domain to use for the messages's return-path

merge.description: whether to evaluate merge tags in the message. Will automatically be set to true if either merge_vars or global_merge_vars are provided.

merge_language.description: the merge tag language to use when evaluating merge tags, either mailchimp or handlebars Possible values: "mailchimp" or "handlebars".

global_merge_vars.description: global merge variables to use for all recipients. You can override these per recipient.

merge_vars.description: per-recipient merge variables, which override global merge variables with the same name.

tags.description: an array of string to tag the message with. Stats are accumulated using tags, though we only store the first 100 we see, so this should not be unique or change frequently. Tags should be 50 characters or less. Any tags starting with an underscore are reserved for internal use and will cause errors.

subaccount.description: the unique id of a subaccount for this message - must already exist or will fail with an error

google_analytics_domains.description: an array of strings indicating for which any matching URLs will automatically have Google Analytics parameters appended to their query string automatically.

google_analytics_campaign.description: optional string indicating the value to set for the utm_campaign tracking parameter. If this isn't provided the email's from address will be used instead.

metadata.description: metadata an associative array of user metadata. Mandrill will store this metadata and make it available for retrieval. In addition, you can select up to 10 metadata fields to index and make searchable using the Mandrill search api.

recipient_metadata.description: Per-recipient metadata that will override the global values specified in the metadata parameter.

attachments.description: an array of supported attachments to add to the message

type.description: the MIME type of the attachment

name.description: the file name of the attachment

content.description: the content of the attachment as a base64-encoded string

images.description: an array of embedded images to add to the message

type.description: the MIME type of the image - must start with "image/"

name.description: the content ID of the image - used to reference the image in your HTML content

content.description: the content of the image as a base64-encoded string

id.description: a scheduled email id, as returned by any of the messages/send calls or messages/list-scheduled

send_at.description: the new UTC timestamp when the message should sent. Mandrill can't time travel, so if you specify a time in past the message will be sent immediately
When the message is scheduled to be sent

query.description: search terms to find matching messages

tags.description: an array of tag names to narrow the search to

senders.description: an array of sender addresses to narrow the search to

api_keys.description: an array of API keys to narrow the search to

limit.description: the maximum number of results to return, defaults to 100, upper limit of 1000

MessagesSearchResponse.description: Array of messages matching the search query (can include both email and SMS messages)

MessagesSearchTimeSeries.description: Time series data point for message search results

time.description: The hour that the statistic occurred

sent.description: The number of emails sent during the time period

opens.description: The number of emails opened during the time period

clicks.description: The number of clicks during the time period

hard_bounces.description: The number of hard bounces during the time period

soft_bounces.description: The number of soft bounces during the time period

rejects.description: The number of emails rejected during the time period

complaints.description: The number of spam complaints during the time period

unsubs.description: The number of unsubscribes during the time period

unique_opens.description: The number of unique opens during the time period

unique_clicks.description: The number of unique clicks during the time period

query.description: the search terms to find matching messages for

tags.description: an array of tag names to narrow the search to, will return messages that contain ANY of the tags

senders.description: an array of sender addresses to narrow the search to, will return messages sent by ANY of the senders

MessagesSearchTimeSeriesResponse.description: Array of hourly message stats matching the search query

mc_template_id.description: the Mailchimp Transactional template ID from the user's account

mc_template_version.description: which version of the template to use - "draft" for draft content or "published" for published content. Defaults to "published". Note: requesting "published" when the template has not been published will return a 404 error.

message.description: the other information on the message to send - same as /messages/send

async.description: enable a background sending mode that is optimized for bulk sending. In async mode, messages/send will immediately return a status of "queued" for every recipient. To handle rejections when sending in async mode, set up a webhook for the 'reject' event. Defaults to false for messages with no more than 10 recipients; messages with more than 10 recipients are always sent asynchronously, regardless of the value of async.

ip_pool.description: the name of the dedicated ip pool that should be used to send the message. If you do not have any dedicated IPs, this parameter has no effect. If you specify a pool that does not exist, your default pool will be used instead.

send_at.description: when this message should be sent as a UTC timestamp in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format. If you specify a time in the past, the message will be sent immediately; for future dates, you're limited to one year from the date of scheduling.

raw_message.description: the full MIME document of an email message

from_email.description: optionally define the sender address - otherwise we'll use the address found in the provided headers

to.description: optionally define the recipient to receive the message - otherwise we'll use the To, Cc, and Bcc headers provided in the document

async.description: enable a background sending mode that is optimized for bulk sending. In async mode, messages/send will immediately return a status of "queued" for every recipient. To handle rejections when sending in async mode, set up a webhook for the 'reject' event. Defaults to false for messages with no more than 10 recipients; messages with more than 10 recipients are always sent asynchronously, regardless of the value of async.

send_at.description: when this message should be sent as a UTC timestamp in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format. If you specify a time in the past, the message will be sent immediately; for future dates, you're limited to one year from the date of scheduling.

return_path_domain.description: a custom domain to use for the messages' return-path

message.description: the information on the message to send

To handle rejections when sending in async mode, set up a webhook for the 'reject' event. Defaults to false for messages with no more than 10 recipients; messages with more than 10 recipients are always sent asynchronously, regardless of the value of async.

MessagesSendResponse.description: Array of send results, one per recipient

MessagesSendResult.description: The sending results for a single recipient

email.description: The email address of the recipient

status.description: The sending status of the recipient

reject_reason.description: The reason for the rejection if the recipient status is rejected

queued_reason.description: The reason for the queuing if the recipient status is queued

text.description: The SMS message content to be sent.

to.description: The phone numbers to send the SMS to.

from.description: The phone number to send the SMS from.

consent.description: The consent type for this SMS

track_clicks.description: whether or not to turn on click tracking for the message

merge.description: Whether to evaluate merge tags in the message. Will automatically be set to true if either merge_vars or global_merge_vars are provided.

merge_language.description: the merge tag language to use when evaluating merge tags, either mailchimp or handlebars Possible values: "mailchimp" or "handlebars".

global_merge_vars.description: global merge variables to use for all recipients. You can override these per recipient.

merge_vars.description: per-recipient merge variables, which override global merge variables with the same name.

async.description: enable a background sending mode that is optimized for bulk sending. In async mode, messages/send-sms will immediately return a status of "queued" for every recipient. To handle rejections when sending in async mode, set up a webhook for the 'reject' event. Defaults to false for messages with no more than 10 recipients; messages with more than 10 recipients are always sent asynchronously, regardless of the value of async.

MessagesSendSmsResponse.description: Array of SMS send results, one per recipient

template_name.description: the immutable slug of a template that exists in the user's account. Make sure you don't use the template name as this one might change.

template_content.description: an array of template content to send. Each item in the array should be a struct with two keys - name: the name of the content block to set the content for, and content: the actual content to put into the block

name.description: the name of the mc:edit editable region to inject into

message.description: the other information on the message to send - same as /messages/send, but without the html content

subaccount.description: An optional unique identifier for the subaccount to limit the blacklist entry

deleted.description: Whether the address was deleted successfully

include_expired.description: Whether to include rejections that have already expired

subaccount.description: An optional unique identifier for the subaccount to limit the blacklist

RejectsListResponse.description: Response containing up to 1000 rejection blacklist entries

reason.description: The type of event that caused this rejection

detail.description: Extended details about the event, such as the SMTP diagnostic for bounces or the comment for manually-created rejections

created_at.description: When the email was added to the blocklist

last_event_at.description: The timestamp of the most recent event that either created or renewed this rejection

expires_at.description: When the blocklist entry will expire (this may be in the past)

expired.description: Whether the blocklist entry has expired

sender.description: The sender that this blocklist entry applies to, or null if none

created_at.description: The date and time that the sender was first seen by Mandrill as a UTC date string in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

sent.description: The total number of messages sent by this sender

hard_bounces.description: The total number of hard bounces by messages by this sender

soft_bounces.description: The total number of soft bounces by messages by this sender

rejects.description: The total number of rejected messages by this sender

complaints.description: The total number of spam complaints received for messages by this sender

unsubs.description: The total number of unsubscribe requests received for messages by this sender

subaccount.description: The subaccount that this denylist entry applies to, or null if none

example.detail: Manually blocked due to spam complaints

labels.description: Optional array of up to 10 labels to use for filtering templates

UrlsAddTrackingDomainRequest.description: Request payload for adding a tracking domain to your account

domain.description: A domain name to add for tracking

UrlsCheckTrackingDomainRequest.description: Request payload for checking the CNAME settings for a tracking domain

UrlsDeleteTrackingDomainRequest.description: Request payload for deleting a tracking domain from your account

TrackingDomain.description: Tracking domain configuration and verification status

created_at.description: When the domain was added to the account

last_tested_at.description: When the domain was last tested for CNAME configuration

cname.description: CNAME record verification status and details

valid.description: Whether the CNAME record is properly configured

valid_after.description: When the CNAME will be valid (if applicable)

error.description: An error describing the CNAME record, or null if the record is correct

valid_tracking.description: Whether this domain is ready to be used for tracking

UrlsTrackingDomainsRequest.description: Request payload for listing tracking domains

UrlsTrackingDomainsResponse.description: Array of tracking domains and their status

UsersInfoRequest.description: Request payload for retrieving user account information

UsersInfoResponse.description: Authenticated user account information including sending stats

created_at.description: Account creation date in UTC YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

public_id.description: Unique permanent identifier for this user

reputation.description: User reputation on scale 0-100, 75+ is generally good

hourly_quota.description: Maximum emails delivered per hour. Any emails beyond that will be accepted and queued for later delivery. Users with higher reputations will have higher hourly quotas

backlog.description: Number of emails queued due to quota limits

stats.description: Aggregate sending statistics across different time windows

UsersPing2Request.description: Request payload for validating an API key with a ping (strict JSON parser version)

UsersPing2Response.description: Static JSON payload with PING: PONG!

UsersPingRequest.description: Request payload for validating an API key with a ping

UsersSendersRequest.description: Request payload for retrieving senders associated with this account

UsersSendersResponse.description: Array of sender information for each sending address used by the account

WebhooksAddRequest.description: Request payload for creating a new webhook.

example.description: My webhook for tracking email events

url.description: The URL to POST batches of events

description.description: An optional description of the webhook

events.description: Optional list of event types that will trigger the webhook

WebhooksDeleteRequest.description: Request payload for deleting a webhook.

id.description: The unique identifier of a webhook belonging to this account

WebhooksInfoRequest.description: Request payload for getting webhook info.

id.description: The unique identifier of a webhook belonging to this account

WebhooksListRequest.description: Request payload for listing all webhooks

WebhooksListResponse.description: Array of webhooks configured for the account

WebhooksUpdateRequest.description: Request payload for updating an existing webhook.

id.description: The unique identifier of a webhook belonging to this account

url.description: The URL to POST batches of events

description.description: An optional description of the webhook

events.description: Optional list of event types that will trigger the webhook

Webhook.description: Webhook configuration for receiving event notifications.

id.description: Unique identifier for the webhook

url.description: The URL where webhook events will be posted

auth_key.description: The key used for webhook authentication

events.description: Array of event types that will trigger the webhook

created_at.description: When the webhook was created (UTC YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.microseconds)

last_sent_at.description: When the last event was sent to this webhook (UTC YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS)

batches_sent.description: Number of batches sent to this webhook

events_sent.description: Total number of events sent to this webhook

last_error.description: Last error message received when attempting to call the webhook

IPInvalidDeletePool.description: Cannot delete default pool or non-empty pool

PaymentRequired.description: A paid plan is required to use this feature.

RejectEmailNotFound.description: Email not found in rejection list or unknown subaccount

RejectPhoneNotFound.description: Phone number not found in rejection list or unknown subaccount

RateLimited.description: Request rate limit exceeded. Retry after a brief delay.

SenderCannotDeleteVerified.description: Cannot delete verified sender domain — please login to confirm deletion

ServerError.description: An unexpected internal server error occurred.

UrlCannotDeleteVerified.description: Cannot delete verified tracking domain — please login to confirm deletion

ValidationError.description: One or more request parameters failed validation.
